title: The 2026 Summer Movie Preview "Boom or Bust" Game
format: Sean and Amanda run the box-office news, then play the 'Boom or Bust' game: guessing the domestic gross and Metacritic score for 50-odd summer 2026 releases. Plus: Daniel Goldhaber on his 'Faces of Death' reboot.

segments.heading: The news
segments.note: Box-office check-in on the way to CinemaCon

films.title: Project Hail Mary
films.note: Strong holds have it heading toward the $600-700M worldwide zone. Cited as evidence grown-up movies with real stars are thriving.

films.title: The Drama
films.note: Also holding well, on its way to roughly $100-150M worldwide (Sean says $138M surprised him). Three weeks running of good news.

films.title: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie
films.note: Still on top of the box office, unsurprisingly. Sean later calls the trailer, which he keeps getting served, 'absolutely execrable.'

films.title: You, Me & Tuscany
films.note: Sean caught a solo Thursday screening. He was the only person in the room. They're holding the discussion for a future episode.

segments.heading: The 'Boom or Bust' game
segments.note: Predicting domestic box office and Metacritic score for the summer slate — in release order

films.title: The Devil Wears Prada 2
films.note: David Frankel returns with Streep, Hathaway, Blunt, and Tucci; tracking $66M opening. Amanda went big at $210M / 68 MC; Sean $184M / 57, comping Thunderbolts and Twisters. Original made $124M (62 MC) in 2006.

films.title: Hokum
films.note: Damian McCarthy horror for Neon: Adam Scott trapped in a scary Irish hotel. Sean $18M / 78 on the strength of McCarthy's Shudder films; Amanda $10M / 62.

films.title: The Sheep Detectives
films.note: Amazon MGM cozy-mystery adaptation with detectives who are sheep: Hugh Jackman, Emma Thompson, Bryan Cranston, Nicholas Braun, Brett Goldstein, Regina Hall, Hong Chau, Molly Gordon. Amanda $8M; Sean bet on old people at $51M. Both 63 MC.

films.title: Billie Eilish - Hit Me Hard and Soft: The Tour (Live in 3D)
films.note: 3D concert film directed by Eilish and James Cameron. Sean went way up on Big Jim at $160M / 71 (comping the Eras Tour's $180M); Amanda $42M / 65. Amanda flags it as the one wild Sean guess.

films.title: Mortal Kombat II
films.note: Karl Urban as Johnny Cage, delayed from last year into May. Sean thinks it loses to Billie Eilish at $137M / 46; Amanda $60M / 44.

films.title: Is God Is
films.note: Amazon MGM, Aleshea Harris directing, with Vivica A. Fox, Janelle Monáe, and Sterling K. Brown as two sisters on a Kill Bill-style revenge quest. Amanda $12M / 74; Sean $11M / 57. A tough mid-May drop.

films.title: Obsession
films.note: Curry Barker's TIFF monkey's-paw horror: a young man wishes his crush into obsessing over him. Sean's seen it and enjoyed it; guesses ranged $15M and $42M, with Metacritics of 69 and 66. Amanda's flagged as the possible breakout.

films.title: In the Grey
films.note: Guy Ritchie's latest operatives picture, with Henry Cavill, Eiza González, Jake Gyllenhaal, and Rosamund Pike. Sean $37M / 52 (comping recent Ritchie); Amanda $18M / 37, having slept through The Ministry of Ungentlemanly Warfare.

films.title: I Love Boosters
films.note: Boots Riley's first feature since Sorry to Bother You, starring Keke Palmer; good SXSW reviews. Sean $18M / 81; Amanda $19M / 64.

films.title: Passenger
films.note: Andre Øvredal's van-life horror with Melissa Leo: a couple pursued by an inescapable demonic stalker. Popped up out of nowhere. Amanda $9M / 52; Sean $12M / 34.

films.title: The Mandalorian and Grogu
films.note: Jon Favreau's Memorial Day Star Wars. Sean went low at $275M / 48, worried it's a TV-tie-in like Marvel's flops; Amanda used Lilo & Stitch math for $421M / 52.

films.title: Pressure
films.note: WWII drama about the meteorologist who picked the D-Day launch date. Sean comped Nuremberg's $14M / 61; Amanda $27M / 61, betting summer light gets old folks to the theater.

films.title: The Breadwinner
films.note: Nate Bargatze's lead debut: a dad minding three daughters during a home reno after his wife wins on Shark Tank. Amanda $35M / 48; Sean, down on it, $52M (on Bargatze's arena draw) / 27.

films.title: Tuner
films.note: Daniel Roher thriller from Black Bear: Leo Woodall as a piano tuner pulled into crime, with Dustin Hoffman and Havana Rose Liu. Sean's seen it and picks it to go viral; $9M vs. Amanda's $28M. Both 74 MC.

films.title: Backrooms
films.note: A24's adaptation of Kane Parsons's viral YouTube series, with Renate Reinsve and Chiwetel Ejiofor. Sean calls it the horror breakout at $67M / 82 (A24's 4th-biggest ever); Amanda $16M / 61.

films.title: Masters of the Universe
films.note: Travis Knight's He-Man, with Jared Leto as Skeletor. Sean's worried the trailer buries the camp; $88M / 55. Amanda $90M, because his childhood is at stake.

films.title: Power Ballad
films.note: John Carney musical from Lionsgate: Paul Rudd as a never-was songwriter in Ireland whose song gets stolen by boy-bander Nick Jonas. Sean's seen it ('okay,' no Once): $26M / 71; Amanda $16M / 74.

films.title: Scary Movie
films.note: The franchise revival with Anna Faris and Regina Hall back. Sean comps Final Destination Bloodlines at $84M / 52; Amanda $52M / 54.

films.title: Disclosure Day
films.note: Steven Spielberg's secretive original sci-fi, tapping UAP anxiety. Amanda went big at $165M / 74; Sean, undershooting to protect himself, $121M / 83.

films.title: Stop That Train
films.note: Adam Shankman comedy with RuPaul, counter-programming Disclosure Day. Sean $16M / 43; Amanda $3M / 47.

films.title: Toy Story 5
films.note: Pixar, with an iPad as the villain. Sean $401M / 89; Amanda $503M (rounding up from Inside Out 2) / 78. A low MC would break the franchise's 84-92 streak.

films.title: The Death of Robin Hood
films.note: Michael Sarnoski's revisionist take with Hugh Jackman and Jodie Comer; A24. Sean $26M / 66; Amanda $12M / 64.

films.title: Girls Like Girls
films.note: Hayley Kiyoko's coming-of-age dramedy for Focus. Sean $9M / 78; Amanda $5M / 58.

films.title: Supergirl
films.note: Craig Gillespie's DCU entry with Milly Alcock. Sean $159M / 50 (comping Thunderbolts); Amanda $190M / 53. Sean's worried it's too violent for his daughter.

films.title: Jackass: Best and Last
films.note: Paramount; the last one did strong, sturdy business. Sean $60M / 56; Amanda $81M / 64.

films.title: The Invite
films.note: A24's Sundance pickup: two couples, one strange evening, with Seth Rogen, Olivia Wilde, Edward Norton, and Penélope Cruz. Sean $34M / 79 (Materialists zone); Amanda $44M / 76.

films.title: Minions & Monsters
films.note: Illumination; the movie-about-movies premise Sean's convinced features minions drinking Negronis. Predictions basically identical at $350-352M; Metacritics 61 (Sean) and 54 (Amanda).

films.title: Young Washington
films.note: Angel Studios' July 3 George Washington origin story, with Ben Kingsley, Andy Serkis, and William Franklin-Miller. Sean $15M / 44 (no Jesus, no Sound of Freedom pop); Amanda $19M / 39.

films.title: Moana
films.note: Disney's live-action remake. Sean $412M / 53; Amanda $410M / 39: 'the reviews on that trailer are bad,' but seven-year-olds won't care.

films.title: Gail Daughtry and the Celebrity Sex Pass
films.note: David Wain comedy with Zoey Deutch. Sean's seen it ('okay'): $6M / 58; Amanda $9M / 71.

films.title: The Odyssey
films.note: Christopher Nolan's Homer adaptation, the biggest movie of the summer with two clear weeks. Sean $350M / 83 (Oppenheimer was $330M); Amanda $412M / 88.

films.title: Cut Off
films.note: Comedy with Jonah Hill and Kristen Wiig: two rich kids cut off from the family money, opposite The Odyssey. Sean $47M / 59; Amanda $28M / 66.

films.title: Evil Dead Burn
films.note: The sixth Evil Dead, directed by Sébastien Vaniček; Sean's birthday movie. Sean $67M / 64; Amanda $60M / 63.

films.title: Spider-Man: Brand New Day
films.note: Tom Holland and Zendaya return, with Bernthal's Punisher and Michael Mando's Scorpion. Sean $580M / 62; Amanda $527M / 51. No Way Home was $814M domestic, but that was a quasar.

films.title: Fall 2: Deadpoint
films.note: Sequel to the tower-climbing thriller, two new climbers. Sean $29M / 53; Amanda (afraid of heights) $7M / 41.

films.title: Ice Cream Man
films.note: Eli Roth horror. Sean's a fan and went $17M / 36; Amanda $9M / 34, since Roth isn't beloved critically.

films.title: One Night Only
films.note: Will Gluck rom-com: Callum Turner and Monica Barbaro fall in love on the one night a year premarital sex is legal, with Molly Ringwald, LeVar Burton, Maya Hawke, and Julia Fox. Sean $38M / 61; Amanda $76M / 52, hoping it fills the July women's-movie desert.

films.title: Super Troopers 3
films.note: Broken Lizard returns. Sean $48M / 52; Amanda $22M / 47.

films.title: The End of Oak Street
films.note: David Robert Mitchell's Twilight Zone-esque suburb story with Anne Hathaway and Ewan McGregor. Sean $81M / 71; Amanda put it in the 'Weapons slot' at $131M / 73, dependent on pre-release hype.

films.title: Paw Patrol: The Dino Movie
films.note: Sean $42M / 39; Amanda $59M / 29.

films.title: The Rivals of Amziah King
films.note: Andrew Patterson's (The Vast of Night) odd Matthew McConaughey drama for Black Bear, out of SXSW 2025 with strong reviews and outside Oscar buzz. Sean $6M / 68; Amanda $11M / 84.

films.title: Insidious: Out of the Further
films.note: A franchise restart without Patrick Wilson: stalkers force a family onto the astral plane. Sean $38M / 54; Amanda $57M / 44.

films.title: Mutiny
films.note: Jean-François Richet's Jason Statham vehicle: Statham stuck on a boat killing everyone, trailer set to KRS-One's 'Step Into a World.' Sean's got a vibe: $38M / 58; Amanda $29M / 42.

films.title: Spa Weekend
films.note: Comedy with Leslie Mann, Isla Fisher, Anna Faris, and Michelle Buteau: 'bad moms' zone. Sean $8M / 39; Amanda $41M / 53.

films.title: Cliffhanger
films.note: RowK's troubled Stallone remake with Lily James, which may not even come out. Sean $27M / 54; Amanda $14M / 33.

films.title: Coyote vs. Acme
films.note: The Looney Tunes movie once shelved for a tax write-off, now out via Entertainment. Sean $36M / 78; Amanda $10M / 72, comping The Day the Earth Blew Up's ~$9M.

films.title: The Dog Stars
films.note: Ridley Scott's post-flu apocalypse, with Jacob Elordi, Margaret Qualley, Josh Brolin, Guy Pearce, and Benedict Wong, from a Mark L. Smith script. Sean $85M / 70; Amanda $61M / 53 ('late Ridley').

films.title: Finding Emily
films.note: Teen dramedy with Angourie Rice and Spike Fearn: a wrong number sparks a campus-wide search. Sean $9M / 61; Amanda $4M / 72.

films.title: Idiots
films.note: Macon Blair's Sundance comedy (formerly 'The Shitheads') with Dave Franco and O'Shea Jackson Jr. Sean $8M / 69; Amanda $7M / 53, though the jokes only get you so far.

films.title: The Adventures of Cliff Booth
films.note: Bonus round: the Fincher-directed, Tarantino-written Netflix Cliff Booth movie, rumored for Venice. As a thought exercise, if a studio opened it wide: Sean $107M / 73; Amanda $95M / 78. Once Upon a Time in Hollywood did $142M domestic.

films.title: Faces of Death
films.note: Goldhaber and Isa Mazzei's IFC reboot, with Dacre Montgomery as Arthur, a killer chasing anonymous notoriety, and a content-moderator lead. Goldhaber traces it from a 2019 Legendary pitch (built around TikTok, which Mazzei introduced him to) through COVID, the writers' strike shooting through their production, and a studio head calling it 'morally despicable' before IFC took it. He models it on Bogdanovich's Targets, not a slasher; its argument is that an attention economy incentivizes mass violence. On the 1978 original, whose real, uncredited director (an ABC nature-documentary maker) he interviewed, he says he can't call it good, but it's an interesting movie that feels like no human made it on purpose.
